feed.title: Mend.io Unified Release Notes
feed.subtitle: Aggregated RSS, Atom, and HTML of all Mend release notes.

entry.title: Mend for Bitbucket DC: Version 25.8.1.1 (24-August-2025)
entry.content:
Unified Agent 25.4.3-179 | Renovate 41.71.1 | Remediate 25.8.1 | Pre-Scan Builder (PSB) 25.8.1

New Features and Updates

  • The First Fix remediation strategy is now available for Python in self-hosted repository integrations. Enable by setting the environment variable MEND_PYTHON_FIRSTFIX_ENABLED to true (default is false).

entry.title: Mend for GitLab: Version 25.8.1.1 (24-August-2025)
entry.content:
Unified Agent 25.4.3-179 | Renovate 41.71.1 | Remediate 25.8.1 | Pre-Scan Builder (PSB) 25.8.1

New Features and Updates

  • The First Fix remediation strategy is now available for Python in self-hosted repository integrations. Enable by setting the environment variable MEND_PYTHON_FIRSTFIX_ENABLED to true (default is false).

Resolved Issues

  • (SAST) Fixed a bug in the proxy configuration of the repository integration which erroneously led to SAST scans not being triggered and a message about incorrect SAST credentials.

entry.title: Mend for GitHub Enterprise: Version 25.8.1.1 (24-August-2025)
entry.content:
Unified Agent 25.4.3-179 | Renovate 41.71.1 | Remediate 25.8.1 | Pre-Scan Builder (PSB) 25.8.1

New Features and Updates

  • The First Fix remediation strategy is now available for Python in self-hosted repository integrations. Enable by setting the environment variable MEND_PYTHON_FIRSTFIX_ENABLED to true (default is false).

  • Introducing a new capability for executing custom pre-scan scripts, enabling users to prepare the environment or perform setup tasks before scanning.

Resolved Issues

  • Fixed an issue where system memory was unnecessarily seized by the Gradle daemon after executing a Gradle script during a scan. Memory handling has been improved for increased efficiency.

  • (SAST) Fixed a bug in the proxy configuration of the repository integration which erroneously led to SAST scans not being triggered and a message about incorrect SAST credentials.

entry.title: Mend for GitHub.com: Version 25.8.2 (07-September-2025)
entry.content:
Resolved Issues

  • Fixed a bug where a scan was able to be triggered before the .whitesource file was present in the repository.

entry.title: Mend Developer Platform: Version 25.8.2 (07-September-2025)
entry.content:
New Features and Updates

  • Improved log-in behavior: Users who are logged out and attempt to navigate to specific Developer Platform pages will now be redirected to their requested URL after logging in.

  • The Developer Platform API now enables using PUT requests to create and replace org/repo secrets, instead of just replace.

Resolved Issues

  • Fixed a bug in the workspace rotation feature where valid tokens were being treated as insufficient.

entry.title: Mend CLI: Version 25.8.2 (08-September-2025)
entry.content:
Resolved Issues

  • (SCA) Fixed an issue where executing multiple dependency scans simultaneously on the same machine would lead to scan results not being displayed properly in the console and platform UI.

entry.title: Mend AI: Version 25.8.2 (07-September-2025)
entry.content:
New Features and Updates

  • (Premium) Introducing a centralized dashboard for security managers to visualize and manage AI security risks. Gain single-view risk assessments, prioritize vulnerability remediation, and track security metrics. Components include AI and project widgets, with enhancements for seamless navigation and detailed risk insights.

  • (Premium) Academic security findings are now integrated into the AI Models table, offering users immediate visibility of model-specific vulnerabilities. This enhancement supports proactive risk assessment, seamless workflow integration, and provides detailed contextual insights, while maintaining existing navigation patterns and preparing for future integration of additional security intelligence sources.

  • (Premium) Added an AI Security Findings column to Projects and Applications tables showing severity distribution (Critical/High/Medium/Low). One-click drill-down opens the AI Models view for the selected item to accelerate investigation.

  • (Premium) Introducing a Severity Distribution widget that summarizes AI security findings by severity. Clicking a severity opens the Projects view pre-filtered and sorted to highlight the most impacted projects for fast triage.

  • Added a side panel in the AI Technologies table that shows the full list of related AI libraries and details without truncation, enabling faster review and decision making.

entry.title: Mend Container: Version 25.8.2 (07-September-2025)
entry.content:
New Features and Updates

  • Microsoft AKS container scanning is now supported in the Mend AI Native AppSec Platform.

    • A new Kubernetes Integration is available for Microsoft Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS).

entry.title: Mend SAST: Version 25.8.2 (08-September-2025)
entry.content:
New Features and Updates

  • Introduced the capability to scan AI-generated code within Windsurf. This offering allows developers to maintain secure coding practices seamlessly within their existing workflow, ensuring code safety without interruption and building greater trust in generated outputs.

  • Improved the performance of processing incremental dumps for PHP.

Resolved Issues

  • Under some conditions, the file timeout was not applied when scanning PHP files. This has been resolved, preventing scans from hanging.

  • In case of a false-positive finding, trying to apply automated remediation suggestions just added white spaces. This has been adjusted so proper error messages will be displayed in such cases.

entry.title: Mend SCA: Version 25.8.2 (07-September-2025)
entry.content:
New Features and Updates

  • Introducing three new SCA conditions for enhanced workflows:

    • Trigger actions for direct dependencies.

    • Detect components with multiple licenses to manage compliance risks.

    • Prioritize vulnerabilities with publicly available exploits for remediation.

  • Users can now group findings in the Findings tab within the Direct Library side-panel. This feature enables better analysis of violations by organizing data into collapsible/expandable Library Rows, displaying aggregated information and individual CVE details efficiently.

entry.title: Mend AppSec Platform: Version 25.8.2 (07-September-2025)
entry.content:
New Features and Updates

  • Added the ability to define custom SLA values for violations in automation workflows, ranging from 1 - 1000 days.

  • The Mend AI Native AppSec platform now supports IP allowlisting, configurable via the Administration > General tab. Administrators can configure access by specifying exact IP addresses or CIDR ranges, blocking unauthorized IPs from accessing the API or the platform UI.
